Title: Food phenolics and lactic acid bacteria

Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Purification from extracts; RT-PCR and Northern blot of putative gene; metabolite analysis of oleuropein hydrolysis
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Hydrolyzes oleuropein to aglycone in the first step of oleuropein degradation, contributing to hydroxytyrosol formation.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Activity partially inhibited by glucose; putative gene expression affected by temperature, ethanol and pH
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. β-Glucosidase initiates oleuropein degradation in L. plantarum. 2. This first step forms the aglycone subsequently converted to hydroxytyrosol. 3. The responsible protein/gene assignment remained partly contradictory in the reviewed literature.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review reports conflicting data: a 40 kDa purified enzyme versus a 61.2 kDa putative β-glucosidase described by sequence similarity.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Converts oleuropein aglycone to hydroxytyrosol and elenoic acid in the second step of oleuropein degradation.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Reported not to be influenced by glucose
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. An esterase activity catalyzes the second step of oleuropein biodegradation. 2. This reaction yields hydroxytyrosol and elenoic acid. 3. The enzyme remained genetically uncharacterized in the review.

Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: HPLC metabolite analysis; cell-free extract assays
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Decarboxylates gallic acid to pyrogallol, producing a high-added-value antioxidant.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. L. plantarum converts gallic acid to pyrogallol. 2. This activity participates in tannic acid and methyl gallate degradation routes. 3. The responsible enzyme was reported functionally but not genetically characterized in the review.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also linked to degradation of tannic acid after tannase-mediated gallic acid release.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Ethyl phenol; ethyl guaiacol; ethyl catechol; phloretic acid; hydroferulic acid
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Metabolite profiling of phenolic acid conversions
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduces vinyl phenols to ethyl derivatives and can reduce some hydroxycinnamic acids to corresponding saturated acids.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. L. plantarum has inducible reductase activity acting on vinyl derivatives of hydroxycinnamates. 2. This activity generates aroma-active ethyl phenols or saturated acids such as phloretic/hydroferulic acid. 3. The enzyme remained uncharacterized genetically.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Only L. plantarum was noted in the review as able to produce ethyl phenols among compared LAB.

Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression studies under p-coumaric acid induction
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with the padR-padA stress response locus induced by phenolic acid stress.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. usp1 is co-organized with padR in the PAD regulatory locus. 2. Its expression increases upon p-coumaric acid exposure. 3. It is implicated in stress adaptation rather than direct catalysis or regulation of metabolite formation.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core common gene because it is specifically summarized within the PAD locus, but direct causal evidence for metabolite conversion is limited.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Tannic acid; methyl gallate; hydrolysable tannins; gallic acid esters
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes ester bonds in hydrolysable tannins and galloyl esters, releasing gallic acid (and glucose from tannic acid).
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in E. coli; purified recombinant protein assay; cell-free extract assay; HPLC metabolite analysis; zymogram
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequence-based identification in L. plantarum WCFS1
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes degradation of tannins and release of gallic acid, enabling formation of antioxidant pyrogallol.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for biotechnological production of gallic acid and pyrogallol; treatment of tannin-rich food wastes; enzyme deployment in beverage clarification and by-product valorization.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Optimal activity around pH 5 and 30°C in native extracts; recombinant TanLp1 reported most active at alkaline pH and 40°C
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. L. plantarum tannase degrades complex tannic acid and galloyl esters. 2. tanLp1/lp_2956 was identified from the WCFS1 genome and recombinant protein confirmed tannase activity. 3. Tannase releases gallic acid, which can be further decarboxylated to pyrogallol.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review notes discrepancies between native extract properties and recombinant TanLp1 characterization; classified as a novel tannase family.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Hydroxycinnamic acid catabolism / volatile phenol formation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: p-Coumaric acid; ferulic acid; caffeic acid
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Decarboxylates hydroxycinnamic acids to the corresponding vinyl derivatives.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Flavoring agents and aroma-active volatile phenols
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene cloning; recombinant expression in E. coli; purified enzyme assay; knockout mutant analysis; substrate specificity assay; structural analysis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables detoxification of phenolic acids and formation of aroma-active vinyl phenols; deletion causes growth inhibition in p-coumaric acid.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be exploited for bioconversion of p-coumaric acid in food by-products into valuable vinyl phenols; important for aroma management and strain screening.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Induced by phenolic acids, especially p-coumaric acid; growth phenotype notable above 3 mM p-coumaric acid and at low pH
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. padA encodes the major L. plantarum phenolic acid decarboxylase. 2. PAD converts p-coumaric, ferulic and caffeic acids into volatile vinyl derivatives. 3. padA deletion sensitizes cells to p-coumaric acid, supporting its detoxification role.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A second inducible phenolic acid decarboxylase is inferred from padA knockout studies but remains uncharacterized.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Aromatic alcohol oxidation / food aroma metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Benzyl alcohol; phenethyl alcohol; cinnamyl alcohol; coniferyl alcohol; perillyl alcohol; geraniol; nerol
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Benzaldehyde; phenethylaldehyde; cinnamyl aldehyde; coniferyl aldehyde
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes aromatic and some allylic alcohols to the corresponding aldehydes using NAD+ as preferred cofactor.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Benzaldehyde; phenethylaldehyde; cinnamyl aldehyde; coniferyl aldehyde
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Aroma-active volatile compounds in foods and beverages
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genome annotation; overexpression and purification; in vitro enzyme assays with multiple substrates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Generates aroma-relevant aldehydes from aromatic alcohol precursors.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for tailoring aroma formation in fermented foods and beverages.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Optimal activity at pH 5.0 and 30°C; prefers NAD+ over NADP+
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. lp_3054 encodes a benzyl alcohol dehydrogenase in L. plantarum. 2. The enzyme oxidizes several aromatic alcohols relevant to food aroma. 3. It prefers NAD+ and can contribute to aroma modification in fermentations.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Substrate range includes some allylic alcohols in addition to aromatic alcohols.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Hydroxycinnamic acid detoxification and volatile phenol formation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses padA in the absence of phenolic acids; derepression/overexpression occurs when PadR is inactivated by phenolic acids or deleted
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: p-Coumaric acid; ferulic acid; caffeic acid
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Decisive switch controlling PAD production and therefore conversion of hydroxycinnamic acids to volatile vinyl phenols
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Controls inducible PAD expression and thereby phenolic acid detoxification and volatile phenol production.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Binds operator DNA downstream of padA promoter; no protein complex established
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Cloning in E. coli; molecular characterization; deletion analysis; expression studies
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Induction by 1.2 mM p-coumaric acid reported
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PadR represses padA transcription in non-induced conditions. 2. Phenolic acids inactivate PadR, enabling strong padA induction. 3. padR deletion causes constitutive padA overexpression.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: padR is divergently oriented from padA and forms an operonic structure with usp1.
